type.fr: Commentaire sur le statut de déclaration de la substance

comments.comment: Note that the value entered is from source testing and from engineering estimates.  Source testing equates to 31.461687 tonnes of NOx emitted at the GEN_J620 stack.  This stack cannot be entered as an individual stack as its average exit velocity and exit temperature are unknown.  Engineering estimates equates to 99 tonnes of NOx emitted from the MILLDBN, MILLDBS and MILL_BLR stacks.  These stack cannot be entered as an individual stack as their average exit velocities and exit temperatures are unknown.  Additionally, there is no ability to enter Stack or Point Releases with different Basis of Estimates.

type.fr: Commentaire sur le statut de déclaration de la substance

comments.comment: Note that the value entered is from source testing and from engineering estimates.  Source testing equates to 48.828127 tonnes of CO emitted at the GEN_J620 stack, and 19.973848 tonnes of CO emitted at the INCIN stack.  Engineering estimates equates to a total of 30 tonnes of NOx emitted from the MILLDBN, MILLDBS and MILL_BLR stacks.  None of these stack can be entered as an individual stack as their average exit velocities and exit temperatures are unknown.  Additionally, there is no ability to enter Stack or Point Releases with different Basis of Estimates.

type.fr: Commentaire sur le statut de déclaration de la substance

comments.comment: Note that the value entered is from source testing and from engineering estimates.  Source testing equates to 0.153969 tonnes of SO2 emitted at the GEN_J620 stack, and 11.737579 tonnes of SO2 emitted at the INCIN stack.  Engineering estimates equates to a total of 0.21 tonnes of SO2 emitted from the MILLDBN, MILLDBS and MILL_BLR stacks.  None of these stacks can be entered as an individual stack as their average exit velocities and exit temperatures are unknown.  Additionally, there is no ability to enter Stack or Point Releases with different Basis of Estimates.

type.fr: Commentaire sur l'élimination

comments.comment: The majority of cadmium is released in the sludge which we land spread. the variation in the amount disposed of is fully dependent on the amount that enters the system via feedstock (wood). It is not feasible to allocate each location where sludge has been spread to determine which section of land received what amount of cadmium.
